subject: Biology
title: Neurons: The Body's Wiring
description: A quick, practical guide to Neurons: The Body's Wiring.

hook.text: Neurons transmit electrical signals at speeds up to 120 meters per second.

buildup.text: Each neuron has a cell body, branching dendrites, and a long axon that sends signals.

discovery.text: Signals jump between neurons at synapses, using chemical neurotransmitters.

twist.text: Your brain holds about 86 billion neurons, each connecting to thousands of others.

climax.text: Every thought, memory, and movement starts as an impulse racing through neurons.

punchline.text: Electricity and chemistry—that's how you think.

quiz.question: How do signals cross from one neuron to the next?
